about.title: About FireYak
about.description: FireYak helps fire departments quickly find the nearest water sources using OpenStreetMap data.
about.openSourceDescription: This app is completely free and open source. The source code is available on GitHub.
about.supportDescription: You can support this project in various ways:
about.dataSourceDescription: All water source data is provided by OpenStreetMap contributors.
about.buyMeCoffeeDescription: If you find this app useful and want to support its development, consider buying me a coffee! Your support helps keep the project alive and enables new features.
about.addHydrantsTitle: How to add hydrants to OpenStreetMap
about.addHydrantsDescription: If you want to add missing hydrants or water sources, please follow the guide in the FireYak README on GitHub. It explains how to use the OpenStreetMap editor and links to the official OSM tagging guidelines.
about.rateAppDescription: Enjoying FireYak? A quick rating helps others discover the app!

error.title: Calculation Error
error.pressureDifference: Output pressure must be greater than input pressure.

authDialog.title: OpenStreetMap Account Required
authDialog.message: You need an OpenStreetMap account to add or edit markers. Do you want to log in?

deleteDialog.title: Remove from OpenStreetMap
deleteDialog.message: Are you sure you want to permanently remove this marker from OpenStreetMap? This action cannot be undone.

hints.location: Tap on the map to set the hydrant's location.
hints.emergency: Specifies that this is a fire hydrant.
hints.type: Specifies the type of fire hydrant. Pillar hydrants are above ground, underground hydrants are beneath a cover, wall hydrants are mounted on or in a wall.
hints.pillarType: Dry barrel hydrants have the shutoff valve underground. Wet barrel hydrants are pressurised at all times.
hints.diameter: The internal diameter of the water pipe connected to the hydrant in millimeters (e.g., 80, 100, 150).
hints.pressure: Pressure in bar, 'yes' if under pressure but value unknown, or 'suction' if a pump is needed (e.g. pond/stream/tank).
hints.flowCapacity: The maximum water flow rate the hydrant can provide (l/min, usgal/min, ukgal/min, m3/h). (z.B. 600 l/min)
hints.position: The physical location of the hydrant relative to the surroundings (e.g., sidewalk, parking lot).
hints.couplings: The number of hose connections available on the hydrant.
hints.couplingType: The technical standard of the couplings (e.g., Storz).
hints.couplingDiameters: The specific sizes of the couplings available (e.g., A, B, C or 110, 75, 52).
hints.operator: The organization or company responsible for maintaining this water source.
hints.ref: An official identification number or reference used by the operator.
hints.loginRequired: You need to log in to OpenStreetMap to save changes.
hints.markerType: Select the type of water source you want to add.
hints.waterSource: The type of water body at this suction point (e.g., river, lake, pond). Helps fire departments identify the available water source.
hints.access: Indicates who can access this water source. Use 'Public' for openly accessible locations, 'Private' for restricted access.
hints.volume: Total storage volume of the water tank in litres (e.g. 50000 for 50,000 litres).
hints.tankLocation: Whether the tank is located above or below ground.
hints.name: A distinctive name for this water source, if it has one.
hints.surveyDate: Date when this water source was last surveyed or verified on site.
hints.awwaClass: AWWA colour classification based on flow capacity. Used in some US jurisdictions to indicate hydrant performance by bonnet/cap colour.

messages.saveError: Failed to save to OpenStreetMap. Please try again.
messages.deleteError: Failed to remove from OpenStreetMap. Please try again.

account.title: Account
account.loginDescription: Log in to add or edit markers on OpenStreetMap.
